summary: Starship is the most powerful moving object ever made
ai_summary: Elon announces a technical milestone for SpaceX's Starship, stating it is the most powerful moving object ever made. This represents a significant engineering achievement in space exploration and rocket development, directly relating to SpaceX's core business of building increasingly capable launch vehicles for cargo and human missions. The post highlights progress on one of the company's flagship products and demonstrates the advancement of their manufacturing and engineering capabilities.
embed_html:
Starship is the most powerful moving object ever made pic.twitter.com/8FpSmjtPo2

text: Telepathic control of robot arms https://t.co/oX21dgBA1z
ai_summary: Elon Musk shares a demonstration of telepathic control of robot arms, likely referencing Neuralink's brain-computer interface technology. This represents a significant engineering milestone in BCI (brain-computer interface) development, showcasing practical applications of neural implants for robotic control—a key product capability that positions Neuralink at the forefront of human-machine interface technology and has major implications for accessibility, manufacturing automation, and the future of human-computer interaction.

text: @aaronburnett Starship will profoundly affect the destiny of humanity
summary: @aaronburnett Starship will profoundly affect the destiny of humanity

parent.text: I find most investors to have binary beliefs on Starship. Either completely assume it’s solved or act as if starship is effectively just R&D. In either case, they hand wave the hard work of Starship and booster teams. Follow this video and you’ll have a renewed appreciation
parent.name: Aaron Burnett
parent.embed_html:
I find most investors to have binary beliefs on Starship.

Either completely assume it’s solved or act as if starship is effectively just R&D. In either case, they hand wave the hard work of Starship and booster teams.

Follow this video and you’ll have a renewed appreciation… https://t.co/joqN8aPzVg

— Aaron Burnett (@aaronburnett) April 25, 2026

ai_summary: Aaron Burnett critiques how investors oversimplify Starship's development status—either assuming it's fully solved or treating it as pure R&D—without appreciating the genuine engineering work required by the Starship and booster teams. Elon's response affirms Starship's transformative importance, underscoring that the technical execution challenges merit serious investor attention beyond binary thinking. This relates to SpaceX's Starship program and highlights a critical business lesson: stakeholders often underestimate the hard technical work required in moonshot projects, which operators must bridge through communication and realistic milestone setting.
embed_html:
Starship will profoundly affect the destiny of humanity

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 25, 2026
